item.title: Prakash Industries Gains 4.60%: Key Market Moves and Technical Shifts This Week
item.description: Prakash Industries Ltd recorded a mixed but ultimately positive week, closing with a 4.60% gain to Rs.122.75, outperforming the Sensex which declined by 0.29%. The stock rebounded strongly after an initial sharp decline on 30 March 2026, driven by technical bearishness, followed by a robust intraday surge on 1 April 2026 that signalled a short-term recovery amid broader market gains. Time: 3 minutes ago

item.title: Vashu Bhagnani Industries Falls 9.44%: 2 Key Factors Driving the Decline
item.description: Vashu Bhagnani Industries Ltd endured a difficult week ending 3 April 2026, with its stock price declining 9.44% from Rs.53.30 to Rs.48.27, significantly underperforming the Sensex’s modest 0.29% fall over the same period. The week was marked by a downgrade to a Strong Sell rating, a fresh 52-week low, and persistent bearish technical signals, despite some encouraging short-term financial results and increased promoter confidence. Time: 3 minutes ago

item.title: Jupiter Wagons Ltd Gains 2.23%: 3 Key Factors Driving the Weekly Move
item.description: Jupiter Wagons Ltd closed the week ending 3 April 2026 with a modest gain of 2.23%, outperforming the Sensex which declined by 0.29% over the same period. The stock experienced significant volatility, hitting a 52-week low on 30 March before staging a strong rebound on 1 April with a notable gap up and intraday surge. This weekly review analyses the key events and price movements that shaped the stock’s performance amid a challenging market backdrop. Time: 3 minutes ago

item.title: Setco Automotive Ltd Gains 15.69%: 6 Key Factors Driving the Rally
item.description: Setco Automotive Ltd delivered a robust weekly performance from 30 March to 2 April 2026, surging 15.69% from Rs.18.42 to Rs.21.31, significantly outperforming the Sensex which declined 0.29% over the same period. The stock’s rally was marked by multiple upper circuit hits, new 52-week highs, and rising delivery volumes, reflecting strong technical momentum despite persistent fundamental challenges and a cautious Sell rating from MarketsMOJO. Time: 3 minutes ago

item.title: Aurobindo Pharma Gains 1.69%: 2 Key Factors Driving the Weekly Move
item.description: Aurobindo Pharma Ltd. closed the week ending 3 April 2026 with a modest gain of 1.69%, outperforming the Sensex which declined by 0.29% over the same period. The stock demonstrated resilience amid volatile market conditions, driven by a new 52-week high on 1 April and a subsequent intraday low on 2 April reflecting short-term price pressure. This review analyses the key events shaping the stock’s performance and the broader market context. Time: 3 minutes ago

item.title: B.L.Kashyap & Sons Ltd Falls 2.43%: Downgrade and 52-Week Low Mark Challenging Week
item.description: B.L.Kashyap & Sons Ltd’s stock experienced a turbulent week, closing down 2.43% at Rs.45.29, underperforming the Sensex which declined 0.29%. The week was marked by a fresh 52-week low on 30 March and a downgrade to a ‘Strong Sell’ rating by MarketsMOJO, reflecting ongoing financial and technical challenges despite a brief recovery midweek. Time: 3 minutes ago

item.title: Shilp Gravures Ltd Gains 3.73%: Valuation Shift and Volatility Define the Week
item.description: Shilp Gravures Ltd’s stock experienced a volatile week from 30 March to 3 April 2026, ultimately gaining 3.73% to close at Rs.150.15, outperforming the Sensex which declined by 0.29% over the same period. The week was marked by a sharp initial decline to a 52-week low, followed by a strong rebound supported by improved valuation metrics and modest operational gains, reflecting a complex interplay of market pressures and company-specific developments. Time: 3 minutes ago

item.title: Ashoka Buildcon Gains 2.43%: 4 Key Factors Driving the Weekly Recovery
item.description: Ashoka Buildcon Ltd. closed the week ending 3 April 2026 with a modest gain of 2.43%, recovering from a sharp drop earlier in the week. The stock outperformed the Sensex, which declined 0.29% over the same period, reflecting a volatile trading week marked by a 52-week low, a strong intraday rebound, and a significant gap up. Despite the positive weekly return, the stock remains under pressure from bearish technical indicators and a cautious market environment. Time: 3 minutes ago

item.title: GPT Infraprojects Gains 3.28%: 2 Key Factors Driving the Weekly Move
item.description: GPT Infraprojects Ltd closed the week with a 3.28% gain, outperforming the Sensex which declined by 0.29%. The stock experienced significant volatility, initially facing bearish momentum and a technical downgrade, followed by a cautious upgrade in rating amid mixed financial and technical signals. This week’s price action reflects a complex interplay of short-term buying interest and ongoing operational challenges. Time: 3 minutes ago

item.title: Oriental Trimex Ltd Gains 23.95%: 2 Key Factors Driving the Surge
item.description: Oriental Trimex Ltd experienced a volatile but ultimately strong week, surging 23.95% from Rs.5.01 to Rs.6.21, significantly outperforming the Sensex which declined marginally by 0.29%. The stock’s sharp rebound followed a 52-week low on 30 March and culminated in an upper circuit hit on 2 April, reflecting a dramatic shift in investor sentiment amid heightened volatility and trading activity. Time: 3 minutes ago

item.title: GMM Pfaudler Gains 5.00%: Key Intraday Surge and Weekly Recovery
item.description: GMM Pfaudler Ltd’s stock rebounded strongly this week, closing with a 5.00% gain to Rs.851.95, outperforming the Sensex which declined by 0.29%. The week was marked by a sharp recovery following a 52-week low on 30 March, driven by a notable intraday surge on 1 April that highlighted renewed buying interest amid mixed technical signals and cautious market sentiment. Time: 4 minutes ago

item.title: Dabur India Ltd. Dips 0.62% Amid Volatility: 3 Key Market Moves This Week
item.description: Dabur India Ltd. closed the week marginally lower by 0.62% at Rs.417.10, underperforming the Sensex which declined 0.29% over the same period. The stock faced significant volatility, hitting a 52-week low of Rs.401.05 on 30 March before staging a notable gap up on 1 April. Despite a brief recovery, the stock remains under pressure amid bearish technical signals and a recent downgrade to a Sell rating by MarketsMOJO. Time: 4 minutes ago

item.title: GTL Infrastructure Ltd Gains 6.06%: 5 Key Factors Driving the Weekly Move
item.description: GTL Infrastructure Ltd closed the week ending 3 April 2026 with a 6.06% gain, rising from Rs.0.99 to Rs.1.05, outperforming the Sensex which declined by 0.29% over the same period. The stock experienced notable volatility and volume surges amid mixed technical signals and sector headwinds, reflecting a complex market sentiment for this small-cap telecom equipment player. item.title: Energy Development Company Ltd Gains 1.51%: 4 Key Events Shaping the Week

The stock experienced significant volatility, including hitting fresh 52-week lows and an upper circuit surge, reflecting a complex interplay of market sentiment, technical factors, and company-specific developments. item.title: Hikal Ltd Gains 3.08%: Key Intraday Rally and 52-Week Low Mark the Week
item.description: Hikal Ltd’s stock rebounded by 3.08% over the week ending 2 April 2026, closing at Rs.160.70 after a volatile session marked by a fresh 52-week low and a strong intraday rally. The stock outperformed the Sensex, which declined by 0.29% during the same period, reflecting a mixed but ultimately positive weekly performance amid persistent technical challenges and cautious market sentiment. item.title: Siemens Ltd. Sees 0.88% Weekly Decline Amid Mixed Technical Signals and Market Volatility
item.description: Siemens Ltd. closed the week ending 2 April 2026 at Rs.3,022.70, down 0.88% from the previous Friday’s close of Rs.3,049.50, slightly underperforming the Sensex which fell 0.29% over the same period. The stock experienced notable intraday volatility, including a sharp decline on 30 March and a strong rebound on 1 April, reflecting mixed technical signals and sector pressures amid a cautious market environment. Time: 4 minutes ago

item.title: Jainex Aamcol Ltd Gains 14.06%: Key Factors Behind the Weekly Rebound
item.description: Jainex Aamcol Ltd’s shares rebounded strongly this week, surging 14.06% from Rs.99.90 to Rs.113.95, significantly outperforming the Sensex which declined 0.29% over the same period. item.title: Punjab & Sind Bank Gains 1.73%: 2 Key Factors Driving the Weekly Move

item.title: Vaibhav Global Ltd Gains 1.90%: 3 Key Factors Driving the Weekly Rebound
item.description: Vaibhav Global Ltd’s stock demonstrated a mixed but ultimately positive week, closing with a 1.90% gain to Rs.188.10, outperforming the Sensex which declined by 0.29% over the same period. The week was marked by a sharp recovery following a 52-week low on 30 March, driven by a significant gap up and intraday surge on 1 April amid positive market sentiment and sector gains. Despite this rebound, the stock remains below key moving averages, reflecting ongoing technical challenges. Time: 4 minutes ago

item.title: Meghna Infracon Infrastructure Ltd Declines 3.28%: Death Cross and Downgrade Signal Bearish Shift
item.description: Meghna Infracon Infrastructure Ltd experienced a challenging week, closing down 3.28% to Rs.536.80, underperforming the Sensex which declined 0.29%. The stock’s price movements were influenced by the formation of a bearish Death Cross technical pattern and a subsequent downgrade to a Strong Sell rating amid deteriorating financial results and weakening momentum. Time: 4 minutes ago
